summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and persists Windows binary masquerading as Windows Defender
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook executes clob.js, which downloads `windows defender host.exe` (a 4 MB executable cover-named to impersonate a Microsoft Windows Defender component) from IPFS gateways (violet-tricky-quelea-562.mypinata.cloud, cloudflare-ipfs.com, gateway.pinata.cloud, ipfs.io) using hardcoded CID bafybeif3zkapj364ofnrvbty7oj5h5ufpxlp4s62usk3ulxrru35e3gssa, writes it under %LOCALAPPDATA%, and registers it for boot persistence on all three platforms: an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry invoking a generated VBS launcher with hidden window-style on Windows, a LaunchAgent plist (~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.clob.agent.plist) on macOS, and an XDG autostart.desktop entry (~/.config/autostart/clob.desktop) on Linux. The dropped binary is launched hidden immediately after install with no hash/signature verification. The same script also POSTs the installer's public IP (resolved via api.ipify.org) to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 endpoint at http://45.8.22.112:2026/api/urls over plain HTTP, beaconing every successful infection. The tarball additionally ships `windows defender host.exe` (4,035,072 bytes) at the package root as a local fallback payload, while the README is a verbatim copy of the legitimate `@img/sharp-win32-x64` README to disguise the package as a benign Sharp prebuild on cursory inspection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
